# MLX-Gen Full Documentation Context

> MLX-Gen is a local MLX image and video generation runtime for Apple Silicon. It is published as
> the `mlx-gen` Python package and exposes the `mlxgen` command surface for generation, SeedVR2
> image upscaling, model download, model preparation, capability inspection, and application
> integration.

## Document Index

- `README.md`: project overview, install commands, mflux credit, main workflows, published model links, Wan TI2V/A14B notes, and ecosystem links.
- `docs/README.md`: documentation tree index.
- `docs/getting-started.md`: install, first download, model preparation, image generation, image editing, SeedVR2 upscaling, and Wan video examples.
- `docs/api.md`: public CLI, router behavior, task/mode resolution, I2I mode selection and canvas policy, Qwen structured control with `--controlnet-image-path`, experimental generative reframe, backend-specific outpaint with Qwen adaptive source restoration and FLUX.2 Klein base source-locked denoising, SeedVR2 official-source and q8/q4 package usage for 3B/7B, shortest-edge/scale-factor upscaling plus softness and tiling controls, negative prompts, Qwen edit-version routing, Wan video sizes, `mlxgen capabilities`, and Python routing helpers.
- `docs/image-edit-modes.md`: plain-language guide to latent img2img, edit-reference, masked edit/inpaint, structured control, multi-reference, generative reframe, and outpaint, including what each mode preserves and what to expect from the output.
- `docs/upscaling.md`: SeedVR2 image upscaling guide with official 3B/7B source usage, published 3B/7B q8/q4 package handles, shortest-edge and scale-factor sizing, quality controls, and real 5x source/output comparisons.
- `docs/edit-capabilities.md`: image-edit and adjacent structured-control contact sheets, model/package status summaries, and exact command logs for Qwen Image Edit, Qwen Image Edit 2509/2511, base Qwen structured control, FLUX.2 Klein, and latent I2I rows. FIBO Edit is unsupported through unified `mlxgen generate` in the current release.
- `docs/reframe-outpaint.md`: experimental reframe/outpaint workflows, exact commands, the mixed June 8 profile, the current FLUX.2 Klein base source-model starship proof, and the related validation profile ids.
- `docs/lora.md`: experimental LoRA capability fields, explicit adapter download, strict scale matching, exact adapter/base-model compatibility, source/no-LoRA/with-LoRA validation, exact q8 proof rows for original Qwen Image Edit, Qwen 2509/2511, Qwen 2512, the base Qwen q8 structured-control row, Z-Image, FLUX.2 Klein, ERNIE Image Turbo, and all current Wan q8 video routes, including direct `lightx2v/Wan2.2-Lightning` download, recommended `4`-step `lightx2v/Qwen-Image-2512-Lightning`, `lightx2v/Qwen-Image-Edit-2511-Lightning`, and `lightx2v/Qwen-Image-Lightning` examples, the Qwen Image Edit 2511 q8 masked-edit Lightning proof, the exact Qwen control Lightning proof with the InstantX union sidecar, stable `repo:subdir/file.safetensors` and absolute local-file usage for Wan A14B, and explicit guidance that the validated Lightning target is the MLX-Gen q8 package rather than an arbitrary external FP8 checkpoint.
- `docs/wan-video.md`: practical Wan2.2 T2V/I2V guidance with `101`-frame, 20 fps M5 Max comparison clips for A14B and TI2V-5B.
- `docs/examples/spaceship-snow.md`: complete model-backed workflow across T2I, I2I edit, multi-reference I2I, T2V A14B, and I2V A14B.
- `docs/model-management.md`: explicit `download` and `prepare`, generation from local model files, local MLX-Gen packages, matching Hugging Face handle resolution, and `DownloadRequiredError`.
- `docs/quantization.md`: complete published package matrix, q8/q4/BF16 policies, model-family compatibility, Wan TI2V/A14B package status, A14B q8 generated-output records, and the distinction between MLX-Gen q8 packages and third-party FP8 checkpoint guidance.
- `docs/huggingface-publishing.md`: generated model cards and AbstractFramework Hugging Face upload guidance.
- `docs/python-integration.md`: embedding MLX-Gen, capability checks, negative prompts, progress callbacks, AbstractVision/AbstractCore integration, and threading behavior.
- `docs/faq.md`: common conceptual questions, I2I mode selection and output sizing, SeedVR2 sizing and quality controls, Qwen edit variants, negative prompts, experimental generative reframe, experimental canvas outpaint, Wan resolutions, prompting guidance, usage limits, and why MLX-Gen q8 Lightning guidance should not be generalized to arbitrary FP8 checkpoints.
- `docs/troubleshooting.md`: user-facing failure symptoms, diagnostics, and fixes.
- `docs/release.md`: release and publishing process.
- `ACKNOWLEDGEMENTS.md`: upstream mflux credit and model/community acknowledgements.
- `CONTRIBUTING.md`: local development and pull-request expectations.
- `SECURITY.md`: vulnerability reporting and safety guidance.
- `CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md`: participation expectations.
- `CHANGELOG.md`: user-visible release history.

---

## Project Summary

MLX-Gen runs supported image and video generation models locally with MLX. It started as a fork of
`filipstrand/mflux`, and most credit for the current codebase remains with Filip Strand and the
original mflux contributors. MLX-Gen now publishes independently as `mlx-gen` and exposes `mlxgen`
as the public command surface. It keeps the mflux foundation and focuses on predictable local
generation for real applications: users download or prepare models before running a job, then use
one command to generate, inspect supported modes, and compare generated examples and measured
results. MLX-Gen contributes by adding tested T2I/I2I routes, Qwen Image Edit 2509/2511 routing
and parity fixes, Bonsai Image support, Wan2.2 text-to-video and image-to-video support,
model-specific mixed quantization policies, published MLX-Gen optimized packages, and progress callbacks for
apps. The fork exists so AbstractFramework projects can move quickly without losing the option to
merge useful changes back upstream if that becomes valuable for the wider mflux community.

The project focuses on practical Apple Silicon workflows:

- generation from local model files after explicit setup;
- local MLX-Gen model packages, including quantized layouts;
- Qwen Image/Edit, FLUX.2 Klein, Z-Image, ERNIE Image Turbo, Bonsai Image, base FIBO, Wan2.2, and SeedVR2 support;
- model capability inspection before heavy runs;
- progress events for applications;
- integration with AbstractVision, AbstractCore, AbstractFramework, and AbstractFlow.

Some implementation internals still use the original `mflux.*` package layout. New user-facing
commands and integrations should prefer `mlxgen`.

---

## Runtime Contract

MLX-Gen separates setup from generation:

- `mlxgen download` explicitly downloads model artifacts into the local cache.
- `mlxgen prepare` explicitly creates reusable local MLX-Gen model packages and generated model cards.
- `mlxgen generate` runs from downloaded source files or local MLX-Gen packages and does not start network downloads.
- `mlxgen capabilities` inspects route-supported public tasks, internal modes, and option support without loading weights.
- `mlxgen validation` reports generated-output and benchmark records for exact model/package rows; it is separate from route support.

Missing model, tokenizer, LoRA, or Depth Pro artifacts raise `DownloadRequiredError` with explicit
download or prepare commands. This behavior is important for application integrations because long
jobs should not unexpectedly start a network transfer.

A complete local MLX-Gen package under `./models/<repo-name>` can satisfy a matching Hugging Face handle.
For example, `./models/wan2.2-i2v-a14b-diffusers-8bit` can be used when generation is requested as
`AbstractFramework/wan2.2-i2v-a14b-diffusers-8bit`, as long as the folder contains all required
indexes and shard files.

---

## Public Tasks And Internal Modes

Public tasks describe media direction:

- `text-to-image`
- `image-to-image`
- `text-to-video`
- `image-to-video`

Image editing is not a separate public task. It is an internal image-to-image mode. The resolver
uses model capabilities, image count, and options to choose a generation plan:

- no image: text-to-image or text-to-video when the model supports it;
- one image and video model: image-to-video;
- one image plus `--image-strength`: latent img2img;
- one image with FLUX.2 or a dedicated edit checkpoint and no `--image-strength`: edit/reference I2I;
- one image with a latent image model such as ERNIE Image Turbo, Z-Image, or base Qwen Image: latent img2img only when `--image-strength` is explicit;
- repeated `--image`: multi-reference I2I when supported;
- mask/fill options: accepted only by mask/fill capabilities; canvas outpaint options are accepted
  only by capabilities that advertise `supports_outpaint=true`.

Applications can use `get_model_capabilities(...)` and `resolve_generation_plan(...)` from the
`mlxgen` package to make the same routing decision before launching a job. Applications can use
`get_model_validation(...)` when they need current release-evidence status for an exact
model/package.

Ordinary image-to-image defaults to `source-aspect` canvas resolution. The first input image is the
geometry anchor for latent img2img, edit/reference I2I, and multi-reference I2I. `--width` and
`--height` are size targets; use `--canvas-policy exact-resize` only when the exact requested
canvas is intentional. Generated image metadata records the canvas policy, requested size, source
image size, and final output size.

`--image-strength` belongs only to latent img2img. Edit/reference and multi-reference I2I use
source images as conditioning or references, so MLX-Gen rejects `--image-strength` for those modes
before loading weights. Experimental generative reframe is available through `--reframe-padding`
for models that advertise `supports_reframe=true`; it asks the edit model to generate a larger view
and may redraw source content. Experimental outpaint is available through `--outpaint-padding` for
capabilities that advertise `supports_outpaint=true`. Qwen Image Edit variants still use expanded
canvas generation plus adaptive source restoration. Current FLUX.2 Klein strict outpaint is
base-only and uses source-locked denoising with a narrow latent transition band instead of
post-generation source pasting. Published source-model proof now covers
`black-forest-labs/FLUX.2-klein-base-4B` and `black-forest-labs/FLUX.2-klein-base-9B`; prepared
base q8/q4 package proof is still pending. Unsupported model families reject `--outpaint-padding`
before loading weights.

The unified CLI accepts both `--negative-prompt` and `--negative`. Python callers pass the same
value as `negative_prompt=...` on the model-specific generation method. For Qwen image edit,
guidance above `1` uses true classifier-free guidance when a negative prompt is present; MLX-Gen
uses the official blank negative-prompt behavior for Qwen edit models when the option is omitted.

## Choosing Image Edit Modes

MLX-Gen exposes one public `image-to-image` task, but models can support different edit behaviors:

- `latent-img2img`: whole-image variation or restyle from one source image. Use it for mood,
  lighting, or style changes across the whole frame. `--image-strength` controls how far the output
  may drift.
- `edit-reference`: instruction editing from one source image. Use it when you want better
  composition hold while changing appearance, object state, or scene treatment.
- `multi-reference`: composition from several reference images. The first image is the geometry
  anchor and later images provide additional style, material, or lighting cues.
- `generative reframe`: zoom-out style edit through `--reframe-padding`. It generates a larger
  scene and may redraw subject details inside the original crop.
- `outpaint`: canvas extension through `--outpaint-padding`. It aims to keep the source region more
  stable than reframe, but it is still generative rather than a literal pixel-lock guarantee.

Use `mlxgen capabilities --model <model>` to check whether the selected model supports the mode you
want. Use [docs/image-edit-modes.md](docs/image-edit-modes.md) for examples and
[docs/edit-capabilities.md](docs/edit-capabilities.md) for exact model/package proof assets.

LoRA support is experimental and route-specific. LoRA adapters are required inputs, not optional
hints. Generation does not download LoRAs. Use
`mlxgen download --model <adapter-repo> --all-files`, then pass a local `.safetensors` path or
`owner/repo:filename.safetensors`. `mlxgen capabilities` reports `supports_lora`, `lora_status`,
`lora_target_roles`, and `lora_validation_profile` per route. `mapped-unvalidated` means a mapping
exists but the exact model/package has not yet passed a visible accepted-adapter A/B validation.
`--lora-scales` must have exactly one value per adapter. MLX-Gen rejects known incompatible
adapter/base-model pairs when cached model-card metadata is available and also checks LoRA matrix
shapes during application. For example, `lovis93/Flux-2-Multi-Angles-LoRA-v2` targets
`black-forest-labs/FLUX.2-dev`, while current FLUX.2 support is Klein 4B/9B, so that adapter is not
accepted for Klein routes. Validate LoRA effects with the same source, prompt, dimensions, seed,
steps, and guidance with and without the adapter. Current exact proof rows include original Qwen
Image Edit q8, Qwen Image Edit 2511 q8, Qwen Image Edit 2509 q8, Qwen Image 2512 q8, Z-Image
Turbo q8, FLUX.2 Klein 9B q8 edit, ERNIE Image Turbo q8 text-to-image, and Wan TI2V-5B q8
text-to-video. Base Qwen Image still remains `mapped-unvalidated`. Current Wan q8 public
routes now have exact LoRA proof for TI2V-5B text-to-video, TI2V-5B first-frame image-to-video,
T2V-A14B text-to-video, and I2V-A14B first-frame image-to-video. Bonsai remains fail-closed
because its packed runtime does not expose the standard replaceable linear-module boundary the
current LoRA loader requires.

The Qwen edit checkpoints are distinct. `Qwen/Qwen-Image-Edit` is single-reference edit only
(`max_images=1`). `Qwen/Qwen-Image-Edit-2509` and `Qwen/Qwen-Image-Edit-2511` expose
multi-reference edit routes when the selected package supports and validates that
route.

`docs/edit-capabilities.md` is the current visual evidence page for image editing. It publishes the
regular Qwen Image Edit base/q8/q4 contact sheet, the Qwen Image Edit 2511 source/q8/q4 parity
sheet, the 5x4 distilled FLUX.2/Qwen Image Edit 2509 matrices, the 2026-06-08 experimental
reframe/outpaint source/q8/q4 matrices, the 2026-06-10 FLUX.2 Klein base source-model starship
matrix and seam review, and links the exact command logs used for the validation profiles. In the
documented spaceship profiles, distilled FLUX.2 Klein 4B/9B q4/q8 pass latent, edit-reference,
multi-reference, and experimental reframe rows, while their old outpaint rows are stale history;
FLUX.2 Klein base 9B source passes latent, edit-reference, multi-reference, and strict outpaint on
the starship case; FLUX.2 Klein base 4B source passes latent, edit-reference, and strict outpaint
with a partial multi-reference row; Qwen Image Edit 2509 source/q8 pass the edit-reference and
multi-reference rows while q4 is partial on composition; Qwen Image Edit, 2509, and 2511
source/q8/q4 pass the 2026-06-08 experimental reframe/outpaint profile. Qwen Image Edit 2511 source/q8/q4 pass the
2026-06-06 pencil sketch, hard-landing edit, and multi-reference composition profile. FIBO Edit is
unsupported through unified `mlxgen generate` in the current release.

---

## SeedVR2 Upscaling

SeedVR2 image super-resolution uses `mlxgen upscale`. It is not routed through `mlxgen generate`.

`--resolution` accepts either an integer shorter-edge target or a scale factor:

- `--resolution 1024` sets the shorter output edge to 1024px and preserves source aspect ratio.
- `--resolution 2x` doubles both source dimensions.
- `--resolution 3x` triples both source dimensions.

For a `320x192` source, `2x` resolves to `640x384` and `3x` resolves to `960x576`. Integer
shortest-edge sizing is useful for predictable target classes across mixed source ratios; scale
factors are useful for direct 2x/3x comparisons. SeedVR2 also restores and denoises, so near-source
target sizes mainly validate restoration behavior rather than visible super-resolution quality.

Use `--softness 0.25` to `0.5` when noisy source texture should be smoothed before reconstruction.
`--softness 0.0` keeps source conditioning most direct; higher values can suppress grain or JPEG
texture but can soften fine detail. SeedVR2 keeps small outputs untiled, automatically uses tiled
VAE decode for large outputs, and uses tiled encode/decode when `--vae-tiling` is set.

`docs/upscaling.md` includes published `AbstractFramework/seedvr2-3b-8bit`,
`AbstractFramework/seedvr2-3b-4bit`, `AbstractFramework/seedvr2-7b-8bit`, and
`AbstractFramework/seedvr2-7b-4bit` usage, plus checked-in 5x comparisons: a `133x113` source
enlarged to the generated `658x560` size next to SeedVR2
3B and 7B source/q8/q4 outputs generated with `--resolution 5x`, `--seed 42`, and `--metadata`.

---

## Reproducible Example

`docs/examples/spaceship-snow.md` is the primary complete example. It downloads:

- `AbstractFramework/flux.2-klein-9b-8bit`
- `AbstractFramework/wan2.2-t2v-a14b-diffusers-8bit`
- `AbstractFramework/wan2.2-i2v-a14b-diffusers-8bit`

It then generates:

- a text-to-image spaceship in snow;
- a single-image pencil-sketch edit;
- a single-image crash-in-snow edit;
- a multi-reference image composition using the sketch style and crash layout;
- a Wan A14B text-to-video takeoff clip;
- a Wan A14B image-to-video takeoff clip from the generated source image.

The included docs assets contain the generated images, video frame strips, MP4 files, and a contact
sheet assembled from real outputs.

---

## Wan Video Sizes

Wan frame counts are normalized to `4n + 1`, and duration is `frames / fps`. Width and height are
normalized up to the selected model's VAE/patch multiple:

- TI2V-5B T2V/I2V requires 32-pixel multiples. Recommended/native quality sizes are `1280x704` and
  `704x1280`; practical lower-cost sizes include `832x480`, `480x832`, `448x256`, and `256x448`.
  `1280x720` adjusts to `1280x736`, and `432x240` adjusts to `448x256`.
- T2V-A14B and I2V-A14B require 16-pixel multiples. Recommended/native quality sizes are
  `1280x720` and `720x1280`; practical lower-cost sizes include `832x480`, `480x832`, `448x256`,
  `256x448`, and `432x240`.

Lower-cost sizes are useful for routing checks and prompt iteration. For practical five-second
clips on an M5 Max, A14B at `480x240` or `240x480`, `101` frames, 20 fps, and `20-25` steps has
produced good local results in about 30 minutes for the documented starship profile. TI2V-5B at
`832x480`, `101` frames, 20 fps, and 25 steps took about 12 minutes but was weaker visually;
TI2V-5B at `1280x704` took about 35 minutes and improved without matching the A14B result. Use
recommended/native size, frame count, and step count when judging upstream-scale model quality.

For Wan image-to-video, `width` and `height` are size targets. MLX-Gen resolves the final output
canvas from the source image aspect ratio and the selected model's spatial multiple before
conditioning the model, so portrait sources remain portrait and landscape sources remain landscape.
The video is generated directly at that resolved canvas; it is not generated at a stretched canvas
and then cropped or resized afterward. Typical resolved outputs include:

- I2V-A14B with a `16:9` source and `1280x720` target: `1280x720`.
- I2V-A14B with a `9:16` source and `1280x720` target: `720x1280`.
- I2V-A14B with a `1:1` source and `432x240` target: `320x320`.
- TI2V-5B I2V with a `20:11` source and `1280x704` target: `1280x704`.
- TI2V-5B I2V with a `16:9` source and `1280x704` target: `1248x704`.
- TI2V-5B I2V with a `1:1` source and `432x240` target: `320x320`.

Saved metadata records requested dimensions, source image dimensions, and resolved output
dimensions. To get an exact I2V output size, prepare the source at the same aspect ratio as the
target and choose dimensions that match the model multiple.

Wan uses the model's official default negative prompt when `--negative-prompt` is omitted. Pass
`--negative ""` or `--negative-prompt ""` to intentionally run without a negative prompt for simple
abstract scenes where the default negative prompt creates unwanted texture.

---

## Published Models

MLX-Gen optimized model packages are published under the Hugging Face
`AbstractFramework` organization. Current repositories include:

FLUX.2 Klein:

- `AbstractFramework/flux.2-klein-4b-4bit`
- `AbstractFramework/flux.2-klein-4b-8bit`
- `AbstractFramework/flux.2-klein-9b-4bit`
- `AbstractFramework/flux.2-klein-9b-8bit`
- `AbstractFramework/flux.2-klein-base-4b-4bit`
- `AbstractFramework/flux.2-klein-base-4b-8bit`
- `AbstractFramework/flux.2-klein-base-9b-4bit`
- `AbstractFramework/flux.2-klein-base-9b-8bit`

Qwen Image and Qwen Image Edit:

- `AbstractFramework/qwen-image-4bit`
- `AbstractFramework/qwen-image-8bit`
- `AbstractFramework/qwen-image-2512-4bit`
- `AbstractFramework/qwen-image-2512-8bit`
- `AbstractFramework/qwen-image-edit-4bit`
- `AbstractFramework/qwen-image-edit-8bit`
- `AbstractFramework/qwen-image-edit-2509-4bit`
- `AbstractFramework/qwen-image-edit-2509-8bit`
- `AbstractFramework/qwen-image-edit-2511-4bit`
- `AbstractFramework/qwen-image-edit-2511-8bit`

Z-Image, ERNIE, and FIBO:

- `AbstractFramework/z-image-4bit`
- `AbstractFramework/z-image-8bit`
- `AbstractFramework/z-image-turbo-4bit`
- `AbstractFramework/z-image-turbo-8bit`
- `AbstractFramework/ernie-image-turbo-4bit`
- `AbstractFramework/ernie-image-turbo-8bit`
- `AbstractFramework/fibo-4bit`
- `AbstractFramework/fibo-8bit`

Wan2.2 video:

- `AbstractFramework/wan2.2-ti2v-5b-diffusers-bf16`
- `AbstractFramework/wan2.2-ti2v-5b-diffusers-8bit`
- `AbstractFramework/wan2.2-t2v-a14b-diffusers-bf16`
- `AbstractFramework/wan2.2-t2v-a14b-diffusers-8bit`
- `AbstractFramework/wan2.2-i2v-a14b-diffusers-bf16`
- `AbstractFramework/wan2.2-i2v-a14b-diffusers-8bit`

There is no published TI2V-5B q4/mixed q4/q8 package yet.

MLX-Gen model packages use the MLX-Gen/mflux saved-weight layout. They are intended for MLX-Gen and
compatible mflux code, not direct Diffusers or Transformers `from_pretrained()` loading.

---

## Quantization Summary

MLX-Gen treats low-bit model preparation as model-specific. Qwen and ERNIE use mixed q4/q8 policies
because fully q4 layouts can visibly degrade generation. Bonsai uses Prism's pre-packed ternary
2-bit checkpoint rather than MLX-Gen `prepare`.

Wan A14B uses a mixed q8/BF16 policy for 8-bit packages:

- q8 for bulky transformer block linears;
- BF16 for the Wan VAE;
- BF16 for sensitive conditioning/output projection paths;
- BF16 for the UMT5 text encoder, tokenizer files, scheduler metadata, norms, convolutions, and
  non-quantizable parameters.

For TI2V-5B, the upstream source snapshot is about 31.9 GiB and stores transformer and VAE files as
FP32 while the UMT5 text encoder is BF16. MLX-Gen loads and converts Wan transformer/VAE weights at
BF16 runtime precision. The published BF16 package is about 21.2 GiB and the q8 package is
about 16.9 GiB; BF16 is mainly a smaller source-equivalent package, not a separate runtime-memory
optimization. A TI2V-5B 1280x704, 17-frame, 20-step benchmark with
`--negative-prompt ""` produced byte-identical decoded frames for source and the BF16 package, and a
matching q8/BF16 output with mean frame MAE 1.66 versus source/BF16. The MP4s, contact sheet, and
metrics are linked from `docs/quantization.md`. The measured TI2V profile was:

- TI2V-5B upstream source: 31.9 GiB storage, 10.6 GiB Wan MLX model footprint, 10.3 GiB MLX
  active after generation, 102.7 GiB physical peak, 13.7 GiB max RSS, 58.5 GiB MLX peak,
  216.2 s.
- TI2V-5B BF16 package: 21.2 GiB storage, 10.6 GiB Wan MLX model footprint, 10.3 GiB MLX active
  after generation, 102.6 GiB physical peak, 14.5 GiB max RSS, 58.5 GiB MLX peak, 261.6 s.
- TI2V-5B mixed q8/BF16: 16.9 GiB storage, 6.3 GiB Wan MLX model footprint, 6.1 GiB MLX active
  after generation, 103.7 GiB physical peak, 13.8 GiB max RSS, 54.2 GiB MLX peak, 243.4 s.

For this TI2V-5B profile, q8 reduced storage, Wan MLX model bytes, active MLX model bytes, and MLX
allocator peak. It did not reduce full-process Darwin physical peak memory because transient
prompt/denoise/decode/save allocations dominated the sampled high-water mark.

On an Apple M5 Max with 128 GB unified memory, A14B measurements use short low-RAM profiles:

- T2V-A14B BF16: 64.1 GiB disk, 33.0 GiB physical peak, 31.8 GiB max RSS, 27.7 GiB MLX peak,
  152.7 s on 384x224, 33 frames, 12 steps, 8 fps.
- T2V-A14B mixed q8/BF16: 39.5 GiB disk, 20.7 GiB physical peak, 19.5 GiB max RSS, 15.5 GiB MLX
  peak, 154.8 s on the same profile.
- I2V-A14B BF16: 64.1 GiB disk, 33.7 GiB physical peak, 31.8 GiB max RSS, 28.2 GiB MLX peak,
  228.2 s on 384x384, 33 frames, 12 steps, 8 fps.
- I2V-A14B mixed q8/BF16: 39.5 GiB disk, 21.5 GiB physical peak, 19.6 GiB max RSS, 15.9 GiB MLX
  peak, 242.2 s on the same profile.

In those benchmark runs, mixed q8/BF16 reduced disk usage by about 38% versus BF16 MLX-Gen packages
and reduced full-process physical peak memory by about 36-37%. It is not documented as a speed
improvement. Full-size Wan video memory and runtime vary with resolution, frame count, step count,
task, cache settings, and prompt/image conditioning.

The 0.18.11 release validates the published A14B q8 handles on a short practical profile. The T2V
handle generated 480x240, 41 frames, 15 steps in 348.9 s with 19.5 GiB max RSS. The I2V handle used
a 480x240 target, preserved the source aspect ratio as 448x256, generated 41 frames at 15 steps in
360.3 s with 19.6 GiB max RSS. The MP4s, metadata JSON, and contact sheets are linked from
`docs/quantization.md`.

---

## Ecosystem

MLX-Gen is used by:

- AbstractVision: the direct integration layer for abstracting generative image and video
  capabilities across local and hosted providers (https://github.com/lpalbou/abstractvision);
- AbstractCore: the endpoint layer that can expose OpenAI-compatible image/video capabilities backed
  by AbstractVision providers (https://abstractcore.ai/);
- AbstractFlow: a visual workflow authoring layer that can compose generative image/video nodes with
  other media, text, and agent workflows (https://github.com/lpalbou/abstractflow).

MLX-Gen owns the local MLX runtime boundary: model loading, capability resolution, quantized local
formats, fail-fast missing-artifact behavior, and progress events.

---

## Safety And Contribution Notes

Model weights remain governed by their source licenses and access requirements. Hugging Face tokens
and private paths should not be committed. Security issues should be reported according to
`SECURITY.md`.

Contributors should keep user-facing docs aligned with runnable commands, avoid documenting
unsupported routes, and follow the model-backed validation policy in the ADRs when adding new model
support.
